channel.title: How to run axios in KOA ctx, to register users? I have code but key gets exposed if I do it in the theme
channel.description:
<!-- 

Are you asking about "how to solve a specific issue/error/problem in your Frontity project"?

If so, please check this guide on the information we suggest you include in your topic so the community (and the Frontity team) can provide the best possible help as quickly and as efficiently as possible

https://community.frontity.org/t/about-the-dev-talk-questions-category/2/3

The info you may provide:
- Description of your issue
- Specific errors you’re getting in the terminal or in the browser’s console
- A repository with the code of your project
- A deployed version of your site
- The URL of your WP REST API

-->

    beforeSSR: ({ state, libraries }) => async ({ ctx }) => {

        console.log("SSR all day long", ctx.status);

      },

//Register user

      // Post request to create a post
    axios
      .post(`${wordPressSiteUrl}/?rest_route=/secure-Oauth/v2/users&${JWT_Key}&${AUTH_KEY_VALUE}`, formData)
      .then((res) => {
    
    //xyz
          })
          .catch((err) => {
            {
         
    //zyx
            }
          });
      };


so how would I go about this? when click register button, it'll take formdata and **submit to ssr ctx** how?

item.title: How to run axios in KOA ctx, to register users? I have code but key gets exposed if I do it in the theme
item.dc:creator: mburridge
item.description:
Alternatively try googling for something like “hide api credentials with a proxy” and see if a solution for your use case comes up.

Read full topic
item.source: How to run axios in KOA ctx, to register users? I have code but key gets exposed if I do it in the theme

item.title: How to run axios in KOA ctx, to register users? I have code but key gets exposed if I do it in the theme
item.dc:creator: mburridge
item.description:
Hi @furrysmile2

Exposing API credentials in client side code can be a problem. We’re working to see what a good solution might be, though it might vary for different use cases.

You will probably need to use a proxy to pass the credentials. A quick google search turned up this video:

Hide API Keys with a Node.js API Proxy + Caching, Rate Limiting and Slow Downs

I haven’t watched it myself yet, but it may point you in the right direction. Let me know if it does.

Read full topic
item.source: How to run axios in KOA ctx, to register users? I have code but key gets exposed if I do it in the theme

item.title: How to run axios in KOA ctx, to register users? I have code but key gets exposed if I do it in the theme
item.dc:creator: furrysmile2
item.description:
beforeSSR: ({ state, libraries }) => async ({ ctx }) => {

    console.log("SSR all day long", ctx.status);

  },

//Register user

  // Post request to create a post
axios
  .post(`${wordPressSiteUrl}/?rest_route=/secure-Oauth/v2/users&${JWT_Key}&${AUTH_KEY_VALUE}`, formData)
  .then((res) => {

//xyz
      })
      .catch((err) => {
        {
     
//zyx
        }
      });
  };

so how would I go about this? when click register button, it’ll take formdata and submit to ssr ctx how?

Read full topic
item.source: How to run axios in KOA ctx, to register users? I have code but key gets exposed if I do it in the theme
